metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the AC4 premise: `docs/user-guide.md` has only a 3-command session-log stub (lines 163-173), not a 'capture-helper section'; update the plan to direct the implementer to first expand that stub into a proper capture-helper section, then add the auto-rotation detail alongside it.
evidence: docs/user-guide.md lines 163-173: only contains 3 bare CLI examples with no capture-helper detail
CLAUDE.md lines 217-242: the actual capture-helper and auto-rotation content lives here
Plan quote: 'alongside its existing capture-helper section'
findings.finding: The plan's AC4 states docs/user-guide.md should document session-log auto-rotation 'alongside its existing capture-helper section,' implying user-guide.md already has a capture-helper section. In fact, the capture-helper detail (`.rebar/current_session_log` pointer, `rebar.append_session_log`, the auto-rotation semantics) lives in CLAUDE.md (lines 217-242). docs/user-guide.md has only a 3-command 'Session logs — durable working notes' stub (lines 163-173) with no capture-helper content. The 'existing' anchor the plan relies on for placement does not exist in user-guide.md.
findings.location: What section, fourth bullet: 'alongside its existing capture-helper section'; AC4 Proving line
scenarios: An implementer following the plan literally looks for a 'capture-helper section' in user-guide.md to place the auto-rotation content next to, finds none, and either places it arbitrarily or fails to write sufficient surrounding context.

The AC4 proving command (`rg -q 'auto-rotat|current_session_log' docs/user-guide.md`) will correctly test for the new content, but the plan's placement guidance ('alongside its existing capture-helper section') is misleading.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'alongside its existing capture-helper section' with 'by expanding the existing minimal session-log stub (lines 163-173) into a full capture-helper section that also covers the `.rebar/current_session_log` pointer and session fingerprint auto-rotation behavior.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the `docs/concurrency.md` `--force` proving command to distinguish the new cascade gate content from the pre-existing `--force-with-lease` text (e.g. use `rg -q 'force.*cascade\|cascade.*force\|--force.*gate\|gate.*--force' docs/concurrency.md` or anchor it to the new section heading).
evidence: Existing concurrency.md line 189: `git push --force-with-lease=<ref>:<old>` — contains `--force` as substring, satisfying `rg -q -- '--force'` today without any new content
`rg` does substring matching by default, so `--force-with-lease` matches the pattern `--force`
Plan's What section requires: '`--force` propagates up the cascade' — but the AC cannot distinguish this new usage from the pre-existing `--force-with-lease` text
findings.finding: The AC proving command `rg -q -- '--force' docs/concurrency.md` is a false-positive verifier: `--force` is already present in the existing concurrency.md text as a substring of `--force-with-lease=<ref>:<old>` (I6 section, reconciler CAS lock description). An implementer who omits the '`--force` propagates up the cascade' gate-interaction content would still pass this AC check, making the criterion unverifiable as written.
scenarios: Implementer adds the gate-interaction section with correct 'gate-exempt' and 'plan-review' content but accidentally omits the '--force propagates up the cascade' sentence — all three rg checks pass because '--force-with-lease' satisfies the third condition.
A reviewer running the proving commands sees all green and assumes the '--force' cascade content is present when it is not.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `rg -q -- '--force' docs/concurrency.md` with `rg -q 'force.*propagat\|propagat.*cascade.*force' docs/concurrency.md` or similar pattern that only matches the new cascade/gate content and cannot be satisfied by `--force-with-lease`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'Design decisions' premise: `idea` status is already documented in `docs/user-guide.md` (§ The `idea` status), `docs/cli-reference.md`, and `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`; clarify whether `docs/ticket-model.md` should cross-reference those existing docs or consolidate/supersede them.
evidence: docs/user-guide.md lines 85-99: '### The `idea` status — a parking lot for undesigned work'
docs/jira-sync-setup.md line 481: '## The `idea` status ↔ Jira `IDEA`'
docs/cli-reference.md line 194: '### `idea`' with full description at lines 197-201
Plan quote: 'only the `idea` status is wholly unhomed'
findings.finding: The plan's 'Design decisions' section asserts 'only the `idea` status is wholly unhomed,' but docs/user-guide.md already has a named section '### The `idea` status — a parking lot for undesigned work' (lines 85-99), docs/jira-sync-setup.md has '## The `idea` status ↔ Jira `IDEA`' (line 481), and docs/cli-reference.md has a dedicated `idea` subsection (line 194). This premise is false, meaning the plan's rationale for what docs/ticket-model.md must carry new content about vs. cross-reference is built on an incorrect baseline scan — the idea status is already multi-homed across user-guide.md, cli-reference.md, and jira-sync-setup.md.
findings.location: Design decisions section: 'only the `idea` status is wholly unhomed'
scenarios: If ticket-model.md is written assuming it's the first doc to cover idea-status, it may duplicate/conflict with existing user-guide.md and cli-reference.md coverage rather than simply cross-referencing them.

The scope rationale ('So the new docs/ticket-model.md carries the agent-facing concept with cross-refs') may under-specify where new content vs. cross-references are needed, since idea is already documented in multiple places.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'only the `idea` status is wholly unhomed' with 'the `idea` status is documented in user-guide.md and cli-reference.md for users but lacks a consolidated agent-facing concept doc; ticket-model.md will serve as that agent-facing reference and cross-reference the existing user-facing docs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the single `rg -c` cardinality check in AC1 with four individual `rg -q` assertions, one per required concept, so each of `idea`, `parent_id`, `discovered_from`/linking, and `set-tags` must appear independently.
evidence: "[ \"$(rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md)\" -ge 4 ]"
Defect taxonomy #5: missing per-concept cardinality assertion when the criterion names four distinct concepts.
findings.finding: AC1's proving command uses `rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags'` with `-ge 4`, which counts lines matching ANY of the four patterns — four lines mentioning only 'idea' would satisfy the threshold without `parent_id`, `discovered_from`, or `set-tags` ever appearing. The command does not verify each concept is individually present.
scenarios: An agent writes a section that repeatedly discusses 'idea' status four or more times but never adds content on hierarchy (parent_id), linking (discovered_from), or tags (set-tags) — the command passes while the AC body's four-concept requirement is unmet.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the count check with:
`rg -q 'idea' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'parent_id' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'discovered_from' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'event-schema' docs/ticket-model.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC3 proving command to also spot-check at least the key semantic terms in `docs/event-schema.md`, e.g. add `&& rg -q 'non-blocking\|Jira\|lifecycle-exempt\|graph' docs/event-schema.md` so content presence, not only the heading, is verified.
evidence: "rg -q '## The session_log ticket type' docs/event-schema.md"
The AC body specifies four distinct semantic properties the section must contain, but the command exercises only the heading string.
Defect taxonomy #4: fixture (heading check) does not actually exercise the claimed content.
findings.finding: AC3's proving command (`rg -q '## The session_log ticket type' docs/event-schema.md`) only checks for the section heading — a section that exists but contains no semantic content would pass, leaving the four listed semantics (gate/lifecycle-exempt, graph/health-excluded, non-blocking links only, never Jira-synced) unverified.
scenarios: An agent adds the heading with a one-line placeholder; make lint passes; the proving command goes green; but the semantics listed in the AC body (non-blocking links only, never Jira-synced, etc.) are absent.
findings.suggested_fix:
```
rg -q '## The session_log ticket type' docs/event-schema.md \
  && rg -q 'lifecycle-exempt\|gate-exempt' docs/event-schema.md \
  && rg -q 'non-blocking' docs/event-schema.md \
  && rg -q -i 'jira' docs/event-schema.md
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the AC1 verify command to assert each of the four required terms appears at least once, e.g. `rg -q 'idea' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'parent_id' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'discovered_from' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md`.
evidence: "Proving: `test -f docs/ticket-model.md && [ \"$(rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md)\" -ge 4 ]`"

The stated requirement is 'documents... the four ticket-model concepts: the `idea` status lifecycle... hierarchy/containment (`parent_id`...), linking (the six relations...)... tags surface (including the `--set-tags`...'
findings.finding: The AC1 verify command uses `rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md` with a threshold of ≥4, but `rg -c` with an OR-regex counts total lines matching ANY term — so the check passes if 'idea' alone appears 4+ times, without requiring all four ticket-model concepts to be present. The stated requirement is that all four concepts (idea-status, hierarchy/containment, linking, tags) are documented.
scenarios: An implementer writes a file that mentions 'idea' five times but omits parent_id, discovered_from, or set-tags — the verify command passes but the stated requirement is unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the single `rg -c` check with four separate `rg -q` assertions, one per required concept term, so all four must be present for the AC to pass.

findings.finding: AC1's proving command uses `rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags'` (alternation) and asserts the count is >= 4. Because `-c` counts lines matching ANY of the four patterns, a document with four or more lines mentioning only 'idea' — and omitting the other three topics entirely — would satisfy the assertion. The command does not guarantee all four concept topics are present.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first AC, proving command
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `[ "$(rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md)" -ge 4 ]` with `rg -q 'idea' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'parent_id' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'discovered_from' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md`.

findings.finding: AC1's rg proving command uses a single OR-pattern with -c and -ge 4, meaning four lines all containing only 'idea' (and none containing 'parent_id', 'discovered_from', or 'set-tags') would satisfy the count check — so the command does NOT verify that all four concept topics are present.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 proving command: `rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md` -ge 4
findings.suggested_fix: rg -q 'idea' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'parent_id' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'discovered_from' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'event-schema' docs/ticket-model.md

findings.finding: AC1's proving command uses `rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md` with a single alternation pattern; `rg -c` counts total lines matching ANY of the alternatives, not one per pattern. A document mentioning only `idea` four times would return count≥4 and pass, even if `parent_id`, `discovered_from`, and `set-tags` are completely absent — leaving three of the four required concept topics unverified.
findings.suggested_fix: rg -q 'idea' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'parent_id' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'discovered_from' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md && rg -q 'event-schema' docs/ticket-model.md

findings.finding: AC1's grep proof (`rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md` ≥ 4) is satisfiable by four hits of the single term `idea` (or any one term). It does not verify that all four named concept topics (idea lifecycle, hierarchy/containment via parent_id, linking via discovered_from, tags via set-tags) are each present as distinct documented topics — the AC would pass if three of the four topics are entirely absent but `idea` appears four times.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the single `-c … -ge 4` check with five separate `rg -q` assertions (one per required term/topic) chained with `&&`, so each topic is independently gated.

findings.finding: AC1's shell proof (`rg -c 'idea|parent_id|discovered_from|set-tags' docs/ticket-model.md -ge 4`) counts lines matching ANY of the four terms, not that all four topics are substantively present. A file covering only 'idea' extensively (≥4 lines) with no mention of parent_id, discovered_from, or set-tags would still pass the grep while missing three of the four required topics.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1 — Proving shell command
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the `-c` count check with four separate `rg -q` checks joined by `&&`, one per required topic term, so each topic must be individually present.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the existing inline session_log description in the `CREATE` event table row of `docs/event-schema.md` should be trimmed to a cross-reference (e.g. 'see § The session_log ticket type below') when the dedicated section is added, to avoid duplicate/drifting content.
evidence: docs/event-schema.md line 46: the CREATE row already contains 'Valid ticket_types: task, story, bug, epic, session_log. **session_log** is a verbose-log type that is gate/lifecycle-exempt, excluded from the graph/health compiles… — see CLAUDE.md "Session logs".'

Plan 'What' section says: 'docs/event-schema.md contains a dedicated ## The session_log ticket type section … and no longer defers those semantics to the root file' — but does not mention trimming/removing the inline CREATE-row content.
ABSENCE: no instruction to reconcile the existing inline CREATE-row session_log description with the new dedicated section.
findings.finding: docs/event-schema.md already contains a partial inline description of session_log semantics inside the CREATE event table row (including behavior bullets and a 'see CLAUDE.md' pointer), but the plan does not enumerate reconciling or trimming that inline content when the new dedicated '## The session_log ticket type' section is added. A naïve implementation adds the section while leaving the dense duplicate inline cell, producing contradictory or drifting content within the same file.
findings.location: What section / docs/event-schema.md CREATE table row / AC: '## The session_log ticket type' section
scenarios: Implementer adds the new section but leaves the CREATE-row inline description intact, producing two locations describing session_log semantics in the same file — one as a table cell, one as a section — that can drift independently.
A later editor updates one location but not the other, silently diverging the two descriptions.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What/Scope section: 'The existing inline session_log bullets in the CREATE table row are replaced with a short cross-reference pointer ("see § The session_log ticket type below") so semantics live in one place.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a task (or AC) to audit existing `docs/*.md` files for any references to `CLAUDE.md` covering ticket-model, session_log, or cascade-gate content, and update those cross-references to point to the new authoritative sections created by this story.
evidence: Plan states 'Out: editing the root guidance file (that is S2)' — so CLAUDE.md retains original content after this story
Plan says event-schema.md 'no longer defers those semantics to the root file' but doesn't verify/fix existing intra-docs references that may still point to CLAUDE.md for these topics
Absence: no AC or task covers auditing existing cross-references in docs/ pointing to CLAUDE.md for ticket-model, session_log, or cascade-gate content
findings.finding: The plan creates new authoritative homes for ticket-model and session_log content in docs/, but does not audit whether any existing docs/ files contain forward-references to CLAUDE.md for these topics. After this story executes, such references would point readers to the (still-unreformed) root file rather than the new canonical docs, creating misleading double-sourcing until S2 lands.
scenarios: A reader follows an existing link in docs/concurrency.md or docs/user-guide.md that says 'see CLAUDE.md' for ticket-model concepts and lands on the old root file, not the new docs/ticket-model.md
After S2 lands and CLAUDE.md becomes pointers, the old intra-docs cross-references are already stale
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: `rg -l 'CLAUDE\.md' docs/ | xargs rg -l 'session.log\|ticket.model\|idea.*status\|cascade' || true` — then update any hits to reference the new sections instead.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan's spec for `docs/ticket-model.md` what 'validate semantics' means in the idea-status lifecycle context — if it refers to the `rebar validate` health-check command, remove it from the lifecycle parenthetical to avoid the pink-elephant antipattern; if it means something else (e.g. a gate concept), define it explicitly.
evidence: Plan text: 'the four ticket-model concepts: the `idea` status lifecycle (dispatch/gate/`validate` semantics)'
docs/user-guide.md lines 204-210: `rebar validate` is documented as 'a repo-wide health check… scans the whole store and returns a 1–5 health score' — entirely separate from the idea lifecycle
docs/user-guide.md lines 85-99: The idea lifecycle is documented as `rebar idea <text>` to create, then `rebar transition <id> idea open` to promote — no `validate` step
ABSENCE: No existing doc describes `validate` as a gate in the idea status lifecycle
findings.finding: The plan's spec text for docs/ticket-model.md uses the phrase 'dispatch/gate/`validate` semantics' to describe the idea-status lifecycle, where '`validate`' is ambiguous: it could be read as the `rebar validate` repo-wide health-check CLI command (documented in user-guide.md under 'validate') rather than as a lifecycle gate step. An executor writing the new doc could produce a pink-elephant error — incorrectly describing `rebar validate` as part of the idea promotion/gating flow — because the word appears verbatim alongside lifecycle-relevant terms 'dispatch' and 'gate'.
findings.location: What / docs/ticket-model.md bullet: 'the `idea` status lifecycle (dispatch/gate/`validate` semantics)'
scenarios: Executor writes docs/ticket-model.md and, following the plan's 'dispatch/gate/validate semantics' framing, incorrectly presents rebar validate as a required step or gate in the idea → open promotion flow
Agent consumer of the new doc believes validate must be run before an idea can be promoted, causing spurious workflow steps
findings.suggested_fix: Change '(dispatch/gate/`validate` semantics)' to '(dispatch/gate semantics, and how `rebar validate` orthogonally health-checks the whole store)' or simply remove `validate` from the lifecycle parenthetical and cover it in the existing user-guide cross-ref.

findings.finding: The plan says to 'fold CLAUDE.md's cascade gate-interaction paragraph' into docs/concurrency.md I4a, but CLAUDE.md's actual cascade paragraph (lines ~61–67) contains only the basic parent-first mechanics — it does not mention plan-review gates, gate-exemption for epics/stories, or --force propagation. Those terms (which the AC's proving regex explicitly tests for: 'gate-exempt|plan-review gate|--force') currently appear in docs/plan-review-gate.md, not in CLAUDE.md. The sourcing label is wrong, which means the implementer may look in CLAUDE.md, find no gate-interaction nuance, and either omit the content or import the wrong text.
findings.location: What section — 'Fold CLAUDE.md's cascade gate-interaction paragraph into docs/concurrency.md I4a'; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' bullet for concurrency.md, replace 'Fold CLAUDE.md's cascade gate-interaction paragraph into docs/concurrency.md I4a' with 'Add a gate-interaction sub-note to docs/concurrency.md I4a drawing on docs/plan-review-gate.md (lines ~50–51, ~222): each parent in the cascade still faces its own plan-review gate; bugs and session_logs are gate-exempt but epics/stories are not; --force propagates to the child but each cascaded parent must independently satisfy or --force its own gate.'

findings.finding: The 'gate-interaction nuance' to be added to docs/concurrency.md I4a references 'parent's own plan-review gate; epics/stories not gate-exempt; --force propagates' — but this interaction rule is not co-located with any existing schema or spec the executor can consult. plan-review-gate.md only says 'bugs and session_logs are exempt'; it does not address what happens to a parent's plan-review gate during a cascade, or whether --force on a child propagates through to bypass the parent's gate. An executor writing this paragraph would need to invent the protocol from first principles.
findings.location: AC#2 / What bullet: 'Fold CLAUDE.md's cascade gate-interaction paragraph into docs/concurrency.md I4a'

findings.finding: AC2's proving command `rg -n 'gate-exempt|plan-review gate|--force' docs/concurrency.md` returns hits (exit 0) if even ONE of the three terms is present. The plan explicitly requires all three nuances to be folded in ('parent's own plan-review gate; epics/stories not gate-exempt; --force propagates'), but the alternation check cannot distinguish one term present from all three present.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — second AC, proving command
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the single alternation with: `rg -q 'plan-review gate' docs/concurrency.md && rg -q 'gate-exempt' docs/concurrency.md && rg -q -- '--force' docs/concurrency.md`

findings.finding: The 'What' section explicitly describes adding a cross-reference to the session-id resolver in docs/config.md when updating user-guide.md ('Cross-reference the session-id resolver already documented in docs/config.md (§ ~429-434)'), but no acceptance criterion verifies this cross-reference was actually added. This is described work with no covering criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC4's prove line to: `rg -n 'auto-rotat|current_session_log' docs/user-guide.md && rg -q 'config\.md' docs/user-guide.md`

findings.finding: AC2's rg proving command uses a single OR-pattern `gate-exempt|plan-review gate|--force`, so a match on ANY ONE term exits 0 — an agent could add only `--force` to concurrency.md and the command would pass, leaving the other two stated nuances (parent's own plan-review gate; epics/stories not gate-exempt) unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 proving command: `rg -n 'gate-exempt|plan-review gate|--force' docs/concurrency.md`
findings.suggested_fix: rg -q 'plan-review gate' docs/concurrency.md && rg -q 'gate-exempt' docs/concurrency.md && rg -q -- '--force' docs/concurrency.md

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
